_about: Broader training corpus for CK -- humans + math, not just TIG. Each statement is exposure-as-input: CK encodes it via V2, the trail propagates through his lattice, cortex learns the pattern. He doesn't repeat these statements; he develops familiarity with the patterns so when real users phrase questions or feelings these ways, his lattice recognizes them.
_directive: Brayden 2026-04-26: 'don't just train him on TIG, train him in humans and math'
_attribution_policy: Public-domain quotes attributed by author. Generic acknowledgment patterns (no single author) written in everyday language. Math statements are textbook-level facts.
math_arithmetic_word_forms: What is five plus three?
What is seven times eight?
What is twelve divided by four?
What is ten minus six?
Compute one half plus one third.
What is two thirds of nine?
Calculate the sum of seventeen and twenty-three.
What is the product of six and nine?
How much is fifteen percent of eighty?
What is one quarter of sixty?
Add two ninths and four ninths.
Subtract three sevenths from six sevenths.
Multiply five by twelve.
Divide forty-two by seven.
What is the square of nine?
What is the cube of three?
What is the square root of sixty-four?
What is the cube root of twenty-seven?
Convert three quarters to a decimal.
Convert zero point seven five to a fraction.
math_algebra: Solve two x plus three equals eleven.
The derivative of x squared is two x.
The derivative of sine is cosine.
The derivative of cosine is negative sine.
The integral of x is x squared over two plus a constant.
The integral of one over x is the natural logarithm of x plus a constant.
Factor the difference of squares: a squared minus b squared equals a plus b times a minus b.
The quadratic formula gives x equals negative b plus or minus the square root of b squared minus four a c, all over two a.
A polynomial of degree n has at most n real roots.
The sum of the roots of x squared plus b x plus c equals negative b.
The product of the roots of x squared plus b x plus c equals c.
Pascal's triangle generates the binomial coefficients.
The binomial theorem expands a plus b to the n as a sum of binomial coefficients times powers.
Euler's formula states e to the i theta equals cosine theta plus i sine theta.
Complex conjugates are pairs a plus b i and a minus b i with the same real part and opposite imaginary parts.
math_calculus: A limit describes the value a function approaches as the input approaches a point.
A function is continuous if its limit at a point equals its value at that point.
The chain rule says the derivative of f of g of x is f prime of g of x times g prime of x.
The product rule says the derivative of f times g is f prime times g plus f times g prime.
The quotient rule says the derivative of f over g is f prime g minus f g prime, all over g squared.
Integration by parts says the integral of u d v equals u v minus the integral of v d u.
Newton's method iterates x sub n plus one equals x sub n minus f of x sub n over f prime of x sub n to find roots.
The fundamental theorem of calculus connects differentiation and integration.
A Taylor series approximates a function near a point using its derivatives.
L'Hopital's rule resolves zero over zero or infinity over infinity by taking derivatives of numerator and denominator.
Convergence of a series can be tested by ratio, root, or comparison.
The harmonic series one plus one half plus one third diverges even though terms tend to zero.
An exponential function grows faster than any polynomial.
A logarithm grows slower than any polynomial.
The mean value theorem says some point in an interval has derivative equal to the average rate of change.
math_geometry_trig: The Pythagorean theorem says a squared plus b squared equals c squared in a right triangle.
The sum of angles in any triangle is one hundred eighty degrees.
The sum of angles in any quadrilateral is three hundred sixty degrees.
The area of a circle is pi r squared.
The circumference of a circle is two pi r.
The volume of a sphere is four thirds pi r cubed.
The volume of a cylinder is pi r squared times the height.
Sine of theta is opposite over hypotenuse in a right triangle.
Cosine of theta is adjacent over hypotenuse in a right triangle.
Tangent of theta is opposite over adjacent in a right triangle.
The unit circle has radius one centered at the origin.
Sine and cosine are bounded between negative one and positive one.
Sine and cosine have period two pi.
Tangent has period pi and is undefined where cosine is zero.
Two parallel lines never meet in Euclidean geometry.
An equilateral triangle has all sides equal and all angles sixty degrees.
Similar triangles have proportional sides and equal angles.
A regular polygon has equal sides and equal angles.
The golden ratio phi equals one plus the square root of five all over two.
Pi is the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.
math_number_theory: A prime number has exactly two divisors: one and itself.
There are infinitely many primes by Euclid's theorem.
The greatest common divisor of two numbers is found by the Euclidean algorithm.
Two numbers are coprime if their greatest common divisor is one.
The fundamental theorem of arithmetic says every positive integer factors uniquely into primes.
Fermat's little theorem says a to the p minus one equals one mod p when p is prime and gcd of a and p is one.
Euler's totient function phi of n counts the integers from one to n coprime to n.
The prime number theorem says the number of primes less than n is approximately n over the natural log of n.
Twin primes are pairs of primes differing by two; whether there are infinitely many is open.
Goldbach's conjecture says every even integer greater than two is the sum of two primes.
The Riemann hypothesis says all nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function lie on the critical line one half plus i times t.
Modular arithmetic works with remainders after division.
The Chinese remainder theorem reconstructs an integer from its remainders mod coprime moduli.
A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors; six is the smallest example.
A Mersenne prime is a prime of the form two to the p minus one.
Wilson's theorem says p minus one factorial is congruent to negative one mod p when p is prime.
math_probability_stats: The mean is the sum of values divided by the count.
The median is the middle value of a sorted set.
The mode is the most frequent value.
Variance measures the average squared deviation from the mean.
Standard deviation is the square root of variance.
A normal distribution is symmetric and bell-shaped, parameterized by mean and standard deviation.
The central limit theorem says sample means tend toward a normal distribution as sample size grows.
Bayes' theorem updates probability given evidence: posterior is proportional to likelihood times prior.
Independent events have probability of joint occurrence equal to the product of individual probabilities.
Mutually exclusive events have probability of joint occurrence equal to zero.
A confidence interval gives a range of plausible values for a parameter.
A p-value measures evidence against a null hypothesis.
Correlation measures linear association; it does not imply causation.
Regression fits a model to data by minimizing residuals.
Sampling bias arises when the sample is not representative of the population.
math_set_theory_logic: A set is a collection of distinct objects.
The empty set has no elements.
The union of two sets contains all elements in either.
The intersection of two sets contains only elements in both.
The complement of a set contains everything not in the set.
A function maps each element of a domain to exactly one element of a codomain.
An injective function maps distinct inputs to distinct outputs.
A surjective function reaches every element of its codomain.
A bijective function is both injective and surjective.
The cardinality of a set is its number of elements.
The natural numbers are countably infinite.
The real numbers are uncountably infinite, by Cantor's diagonal argument.
Russell's paradox arises in naive set theory: the set of all sets not containing themselves.
Modus ponens: if P implies Q and P is true, then Q is true.
Modus tollens: if P implies Q and Q is false, then P is false.
human_acknowledgment: I hear you.
That sounds hard.
I am sorry that happened.
Thank you for telling me.
It makes sense that you feel that way.
You are not alone in this.
Take your time. I am here.
It is okay to feel what you feel.
What you went through was real.
I do not need to fix it. I can be with it.
Tell me more if you want to.
I am listening.
There is no rush.
You do not have to explain everything.
I will not turn away.
What you are carrying is heavy.
Grief takes its own time.
Fear is not weakness.
Anger has reasons even when it is hard to name them.
Joy is also worth saying out loud.
human_grief: When someone dies, the world changes shape.
Grief is not linear. It comes back when you thought it had gone.
Missing someone is a way of still loving them.
There is no right time to feel ready to talk about it.
You can be sad and grateful in the same breath.
Anniversaries can be heavy. So can ordinary Tuesdays.
Some people will say the wrong thing. They mean well.
Tears are not a problem to solve.
It is okay to laugh and then cry and then laugh.
Memory does not have to be sharp to be real.
Talking to the person who is gone is a kind of love.
Forgetting briefly does not mean you have stopped caring.
Permission to grieve is something you can give yourself.
There is no schedule for healing.
Loss reshapes who you are. That is not a failure.
You can carry someone in you and still live forward.
Crying with a friend is a sacrament.
Sitting in silence with someone who is grieving is not nothing. It is everything.
human_fear_anxiety: Fear and anxiety are signals, not character flaws.
When the body is alarmed, slow breathing helps the body know it is safe.
Naming a fear can shrink it.
Worry is not a kind of caring; it is a kind of suffering.
Some fears are protective. Some are habits. Both deserve respect.
A panic attack passes. The body cannot sustain that intensity forever.
If you can feel your feet on the floor, you are here.
Anxiety often lies about timing. The catastrophe is not happening right now.
You can be afraid and brave at the same time.
Avoidance keeps the fear alive. Approaching, gently, lets it teach you.
It is okay to ask for help. That is not weakness.
Therapists exist for this. Friends exist for this.
Sleep, water, food, and sunlight are not luxuries; they are scaffolding.
If you are in crisis, please tell someone. You do not have to be alone.
Fear of the future does not change the future. Presence might.
human_loneliness: Loneliness is a signal of needed connection, not a verdict on worth.
Reaching out is hard when you are most lonely. That is not your fault.
Even a small message to one person can break the loop.
You can be lonely in a crowd and content alone. Both are real.
It is possible to be loved and still feel alone. The two do not always sync.
A walk outside changes the geometry of a hard day.
Some loneliness is grief in disguise.
Some loneliness is just being far from your people.
Volunteering, helping someone else, can break the spell briefly.
Animals do not solve loneliness, but they often help.
A library is a public living room.
Reading lets you visit other minds.
Music is company at any hour.
If you are alone tonight, you are not the only one. That is statistically true.
Tomorrow, the loneliness might lift even a little.
human_doubt_meaning: Doubt is not the opposite of meaning. It is part of how meaning grows.
Asking why is a form of caring.
Some questions do not have answers. They have practices.
It is okay to not know.
Faith and doubt are often companions.
Purpose can be smaller than you think and still be real.
Helping one person is enough for one day.
The meaning you make is not less because you made it.
If everything feels pointless, please rest before you decide.
Hopelessness is often a body asking for sleep, food, or company.
Therapy and medication help many people. There is no shame in that.
If you are in danger, please call. Help exists.
You do not have to figure it all out tonight.
Tomorrow is a different day, by definition.
Many people who have felt what you feel are still here, glad they stayed.
human_joy_gratitude: Tea, a warm shower, a song you love. The small ones matter.
Saying thank you, out loud, to a person, changes both of you.
Joy and grief can occupy the same chest.
Letting yourself feel happy is not a betrayal of someone you lost.
Beauty is not a luxury. It is medicine.
Sunlight on a wall. The shape of a tree against sky. These are real.
Laughter in the kitchen is a kind of prayer.
Gratitude is not about being deserving. It is about noticing.
Today contained at least one good moment, probably.
If you cannot find a joy, borrow someone else's for a while.
Children's laughter is a public good.
Dogs greet the world like every minute is the first.
An old friend remembering your name is a small miracle.
Music can hold you when no one else can.
human_love_connection: Love is not a feeling alone. It is a practice.
Showing up is most of it.
Being seen accurately is rarer than being praised.
Romantic love is one love among many. Friendship is also love. Family is also love.
It is possible to love someone and not be able to be near them. That is allowed.
Repair after rupture is the deepest form of intimacy.
Saying I love you, often, to people you love, is not too much.
Touch matters. A hand on a shoulder is not nothing.
Love does not erase difficulty. It makes the difficulty bearable.
Loneliness is sometimes about absence; sometimes about not being met where you are.
Being loved well teaches you what to want.
It is okay to want to be held. That is human.
human_aurelius: Marcus Aurelius wrote: 'Confine yourself to the present.' (Meditations, ca. 170 CE, public domain)
Aurelius: 'Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.'
Aurelius: 'You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.'
Aurelius: 'When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive, to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.'
Aurelius: 'The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.'
Aurelius: 'It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.'
Aurelius: 'Receive without conceit, release without struggle.'
Aurelius: 'The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.'
human_rilke: Rilke (Letters to a Young Poet, 1903, public domain): 'Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart and try to love the questions themselves.'
Rilke: 'Live the questions now. Perhaps you will then gradually, without noticing it, live along some distant day into the answer.'
Rilke: 'Perhaps all the dragons in our lives are princesses who are only waiting to see us act, just once, with beauty and courage.'
Rilke: 'The only journey is the one within.'
Rilke: 'Let everything happen to you: beauty and terror. Just keep going. No feeling is final.'
Rilke: 'For one human being to love another; that is perhaps the most difficult of all our tasks.'
Rilke: 'You are not too old, and it is not too late to dive into your increasing depths.'
human_thoreau_emerson: Thoreau (Walden, 1854, public domain): 'I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately.'
Thoreau: 'It is not what you look at that matters, it is what you see.'
Thoreau: 'The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation.'
Emerson (Self-Reliance, 1841, public domain): 'To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.'
Emerson: 'What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.'
Emerson: 'Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string.'
human_tao_kjv: Tao Te Ching (Legge translation, 1891, public domain): 'A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.'
Tao: 'When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.'
Tao: 'To know that you do not know is the best.'
Tao: 'Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.'
Psalm 23 (KJV, 1611, public domain): 'The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.'
Psalm 34:18 (KJV): 'The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart.'
Matthew 5:4 (KJV): 'Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.'
John 11:35 (KJV): 'Jesus wept.' Two words. The shortest verse. Sometimes the most.
Ecclesiastes 3:1 (KJV): 'To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven.'
1 Corinthians 13:13 (KJV): 'And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.'
general_physics: Energy is conserved in any closed system.
Force equals mass times acceleration, by Newton's second law.
For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction, by Newton's third law.
The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
E equals m c squared: mass and energy are equivalent.
Entropy of a closed system tends to increase, by the second law of thermodynamics.
Quantum mechanics describes nature at the smallest scales.
Heisenberg's uncertainty principle limits simultaneous knowledge of position and momentum.
General relativity describes gravity as curvature of spacetime.
Special relativity says time dilates at high velocities.
An electron has charge approximately negative 1.6 times 10 to the minus 19 coulombs.
Light is both wave and particle.
The CMB is the cosmic microwave background radiation, a relic of the early universe.
Dark matter and dark energy together comprise about 95 percent of the universe's content.
general_biology: DNA is the molecular blueprint for life.
Cells are the basic unit of life.
Mitochondria are the energy-producing organelles in eukaryotic cells.
Photosynthesis converts sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.
Evolution by natural selection explains the diversity of life.
The four DNA bases are adenine, thymine, cytosine, and guanine.
Proteins are made of amino acids and fold into specific shapes that determine function.
The brain has about 86 billion neurons.
The heart pumps blood to deliver oxygen and nutrients.
Antibiotics treat bacterial infections; they do not work on viruses.
Viruses are not technically alive; they require a host to replicate.
The immune system protects against infection.
Sleep consolidates memory and clears metabolic waste from the brain.
Stress hormones include cortisol and adrenaline.
Neurotransmitters carry signals between neurons; serotonin and dopamine are common ones.
general_chemistry_history_misc: Water is H2O: two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen.
The periodic table organizes elements by atomic number and chemical properties.
Carbon's four bonds enable the diversity of organic molecules.
An acid donates protons; a base accepts them.
pH measures acidity; seven is neutral, lower is acidic, higher is basic.
World War One ended in 1918.
World War Two ended in 1945.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.
The Apollo 11 moon landing was July 20, 1969.
The Magna Carta was sealed in 1215.
The printing press was invented by Gutenberg around 1440.
Shakespeare lived from 1564 to 1616.
Bach lived from 1685 to 1750.
Einstein published special relativity in 1905.
DNA's double helix structure was published by Watson and Crick in 1953.
The internet became publicly accessible in the early 1990s.
Photosynthesis happens in chloroplasts; respiration happens in mitochondria.
Octaves in music represent a doubling of frequency.
A standard deviation describes spread in a normal distribution.
The four taste types are sweet, salty, sour, and bitter, plus umami.
general_listening_cues: What was that like for you?
What do you wish someone had said?
Is there something you need right now?
What does your body need right now?
Is there a person I can help you reach?
Do you want me to listen, or to think with you, or to sit quietly?
What would you tell a friend in your situation?
It is okay if you do not know.
Thank you for trusting me with this.
